sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content: "Limited role in cutaneous lymphomas; primarily for diagnosis (biopsy) and for localized, small lesions where excision is feasible and non-disfiguring.", "principles": ["Surgical excision may be considered for solitary, small lesions of indolent PCBCL (PCMZL, PCFCL) or for localized PC-ALCL when RT is not desired or feasible.", "Excision is generally not curative and is associated with high recurrence rates, especially for indolent subtypes.", "Surgical margin should be minimal to avoid disfigurement, especially in cosmetically sensitive areas."], "procedures": [{"name": "Surgical excision", "approach": "Simple excision with minimal margins (1-2 cm) for small, solitary lesions.", "indications": "Small, solitary lesions of PCMZL, PCFCL, or PC-ALCL where RT is not preferred.", "key_complications": "Scarring, recurrence at excision site.", "operative_mortality": "Negligible."}], "resectability_criteria": "Lesions must be solitary, small, and located in areas where excision is feasible without significant morbidity or disfigurement. Not recommended for multifocal, large, or anatomically challenging lesions.", "pre_operative_optimisation": "Standard pre-operative evaluation; ensure adequate biopsy has been performed for diagnosis."

cards.name: Procedure
cards.detail: {"name": "Surgical excision", "approach": "Simple excision with minimal margins (1-2 cm) for small, solitary lesions.", "indications": "Small, solitary lesions of PCMZL, PCFCL, or PC-ALCL where RT is not preferred.", "key_complications": "Scarring, recurrence at excision site.", "operative_mortality": "Negligible."}
